title: Cirrus: Run system & integration tests in parallel
body:
Given that flakes inevitably occur as testing grows wider, position the system tests in parallel with the integration tests as much as possible. The thinking here is, flaking sooner is better than later.  This is because it provides an earlier opportunity for developers to re-run tasks.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
LGTM
@cevich Could you remove the draft if this is ready to merge?
/approve
/lgtm

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> reduces the overall time to merge

Maybe?  Cirrus-CI reports wall-clock runtime at 1:49.  Some random other PR I just looked at seems to have finished in 1:37 :confused: 

Edit: Yet another I looked at says 2:23, so it seems there's quite a bit of variability.  I didn't examine either of these examples for task-reruns.
force-push: rebased.  Gathering more runtime data on another full run.

Just observed github reporting and Cirrus-CI actually running all the int and sys tasks at the same time.  So that's a win, there doesn't appear to be any queuing for VM-based tasks.  How well that scales to 5 or 10 simultaneous PRs remains to be seen.
Okay, I'm convinced now.  This PR does what it says on the can.
